nativeRange.habitat: Intertidal to shallow subtidal rocky substrate, reef flats, seagrass meadows, and mangrove roots; generally found just below the low-tide line

avoidWith: dedicated herbivorous fish (tangs, rabbitfish) in small tanks that may graze the lightly-calcified fronds

imageCandidates.notes: Narrissa Spies; 4608x3456. Direct Padina sanctae-crucis match, tack-sharp; dense cluster of scroll-curling fan fronds with concentric banding, gold/yellow/brown color and whitened lightly-calcified upper surfaces. Best diagnostic display.

Dan Schofield (via iNaturalist); 2048x1274. In-water single scroll frond showing the classic spiral scroll form with concentric banding and yellow/gold margin very clearly. Padina genus ID certain, species inferred (macroalgae look-alike).

Nick T. (via iNaturalist); 1536x2048. In-water tidepool colony of many scroll fans with gold/yellow margins and white banded surfaces; shows the natural growth habit. Species inferred within Padina (look-alike).

summary: Golden Scroll is a tropical brown macroalga with fan-shaped, scroll-curling fronds banded in gold, yellow, and brown, with lightly calcified whitened upper surfaces. A shallow-water species suited to reef aquariums with moderate to strong lighting and good water flow.
careNotes:
Golden Scroll (Padina sanctae-crucis) is a tropical brown macroalga in the family Dictyotaceae — one of the few calcareous genera among brown algae. Its thallus grows as a thin, fan- or scroll-shaped frond with characteristic concentric banding, typical of the Padina genus. The upper surfaces are lightly calcified and whitened, while the lower portions display the gold, yellow, and brown coloration that gives this species its common name; the colors tend to appear richer under strong full-spectrum reef lighting. The type locality is St. Croix, US Virgin Islands, and the species has a broad tropical distribution spanning the Caribbean, Gulf of Mexico, the Atlantic coast of South America, and into the Indo-Pacific.

As a shallow intertidal species — typically found from the surface down to around 12 m — Golden Scroll tends to do best under medium to strong aquarium lighting. Hobbyists experiencing slow decline may benefit from increasing light intensity. Growth rate is slow, consistent with the calcified, intertidal growth habit of the Dictyotaceae family. Moderate to strong flow is generally recommended.

Golden Scroll is lightly calcified, depositing a thin layer of calcium carbonate on its fronds. This may contribute minimally to alkalinity and calcium demand in the tank; regular calcium dosing or a calcium reactor is generally advised in reef systems where this species is kept. The lightly calcified fronds can occasionally be grazed by dedicated herbivores such as tangs and rabbitfish; most standard reef fish and invertebrates tend to leave them undisturbed.

**ATTACHMENT WARNING:** Golden Scroll will deteriorate at its base if buried in a sand bed. It should be attached to rockwork rather than planted in substrate — suitable methods include aquarium-safe superglue, zip-tie or fishing line secured to a plug or rock, or wedging between rock crevices.
breedingNotes: Propagation in captivity is generally accomplished by fragmentation — separating healthy frond sections and attaching them to rockwork or a plug. The species also reproduces via sporulation (asexual spore production) in the wild, with spores dispersing via water movement to settle on hard substrates. Sexual reproduction via alternating gametophyte–sporophyte lifecycle (typical of brown algae) occurs naturally but has not been documented in hobby aquaria.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Primary hobby-trade source for this specialty form. Provides species identification (Padina sanctae-crucis), trade name (Golden Brown Scroll), morphology description (ruffled fan/scroll form, gold/yellow/brown coloration with white accents), and general reef compatibility context. No explicit water parameter data.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Padina sanctae-crucis
additional.notes: Used for taxonomic classification (Dictyotaceae / Dictyotales / Phaeophyceae), morphology (curled fanlike branches ~15 cm, calcified whitened upper surface), native range (tropical worldwide, Caribbean, Florida, Brazil, South Pacific), habitat (intertidal to ~10 m, rocky substrate, reef flats, seagrass meadows, mangrove roots), and reproduction (sexual + asexual sporulation).

additional.name: Reef Cleaners — Scroll Algae
additional.notes: Cross-reference for aquarium husbandry. States 'strong to intense' lighting required due to shallow native habitat; notes lightly calcified fronds susceptible to herbivore grazing; mentions habitat as submerged rocks and mangrove roots just below the low-tide line.

additional.name: Marine Plant Book — Padina
additional.notes: Cross-reference for care parameters. Lighting 'moderate to high'; temperature 78–86°F (25.6–30°C); max height 6 in (~15 cm); growth rate 'slow'; native range Gulf of Mexico/Caribbean/Atlantic at 3–40 ft (0–12 m); propagation by fragmentation and sporulation; nutrient uptake 'fair'; requires calcium supplementation; rated 'advanced' difficulty.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
